text:
This Tuesday at 2pm ET, the destinies of four teams will intertwine at the iconic Stade Geoffroy-Guichard in Saint-Ãtienne for the last and decisive round of Group A.

The United States, who entered the Olympic Games with an air of confidence, were harshly brought back to reality when France trounced them with a resounding 3-0 in their opening match. Talk about a wake-up call, right? In the second round, the US demolished New Zealand 4-1, securing not only three precious points but also a goal difference that could prove crucial.

Follow MLS Multiplex on X (Twitter).

And what can we say about the young Kevin Paredes? This midfielder, playing for Wolfsburg in the Bundesliga, has been a true maestro on the field. With precise passes and game vision worthy of a veteran, Paredes not only led the victory against New Zealand but also injected hope into a team that seemed destined for failure.

On the other side of the field, we have the Guinea team, facing an almost apocalyptic scenario. Two consecutive defeatsâa painful opener against New Zealand, losing 2-1, and a more subtle yet equally devastating blow against France, who emerged victorious 1-0. Guinea's hopes rest on the shoulders of Amadou Diawara, an experienced midfielder who plays for Belgium's Anderlecht. But can one man carry the weight of an Olympic dream? It sounds like a mission impossible, but who knows?

The other match between New Zealand and France is also of interest to us! New Zealand, a team that started with a sparkle in their eyes after defeating Guinea, but had their hopes momentarily dimmed by the loss to the United States, faces the host French team, who need no introduction. France has been a true fortress on the field, and playing at home with fervent support, it's hard to imagine a scenario where they come out defeated. France has shown why they are considered favorites. With a dominant performance against the United States and a solid victory over Guinea, the French are cruising smoothly toward the next phase.

The United States has a chance to continue their path to redemption, while Guinea needs a miracleâor two. Kevin Paredes and Amadou Diawara will be the names to watch. Two players at different stages of their careers, but both carrying the weight of their nations' expectations. The young American with all his potential yet to be unleashed, and the experienced Guinean, fighting to keep his team's flame alive.

title: USA Faces an Almost Impossible Mission Against Guinea at the Paris Olympics
description: This Tuesday at 2pm ET, the crucial Group A match at Stade Geoffroy-Guichard will determine the fates of the USA and Guinea. Can Kevin Paredes lead the USA to victory or will Diawara's Guinea prevail?
